lesson: c3-l06 / Ethics of AI in Education
title: Ethics of AI in Education

scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: Ethics of AI in education. [pause] Bias, fairness, and human agency.

scenes.name: Why
scenes.text: Ethics is not separate from technology. [pause] Every AI tool you use in class carries ethical weight — for real students, in real classrooms. [pause] Understanding it is a professional responsibility.

scenes.name: Bias
scenes.text: Algorithmic bias is the first concern. [pause] AI learns from data — and that data carries the world's inequalities. [pause] Stereotyped examples. Underrepresented groups. Subtle exclusion. [pause] Always review before classroom use.

scenes.name: Oracle
scenes.text: The <emphasis>oracle effect</emphasis>. [pause] Students trust computers more than they should. [pause] AI presents wrong answers with the same confidence as right ones. [pause] Make AI errors visible — make them a teaching moment.

scenes.name: Privacy
scenes.text: Data privacy. [pause] Every interaction is recorded. Every name, every detail, every story typed in. [pause] Under GDPR — that matters. [pause] Teach students never to share personal information with AI.

scenes.name: Agency
scenes.text: Human agency is the deepest principle. [pause] AI provides information, suggestions, tools. [pause] But the pedagogical decision — what to teach, how, when — belongs to the teacher. Never to the technology.

scenes.name: Misuses
scenes.text: Common ethical misuses to watch for. [pause] Generating quizzes without checking for bias. Trusting AI grades as final. Pasting student data into public tools. [pause] All preventable. None acceptable.

scenes.name: Literacy
scenes.text: Use ethics as a teaching tool. [pause] Analyse a biased example. Discuss a hallucination. Explore a privacy question. [pause] Critical thinking about AI is critical thinking, full stop.

scenes.name: Closing
scenes.text: Technology serves learning. [pause] Not the other way around.
